description: Late luteal, low sleep, high stress, mood dropping

hormoneContext.narrativeContext: Both estrogen and progesterone are typically declining toward their monthly low, which can amplify emotional sensitivity and physical discomfort. This hormone drop is often what drives pre-period symptoms.

reasoning: No strong personal baseline deviation detected
Insight priority drivers: stress_trend_spiking, sleep_trend_declining, sleep_stress_amplification, mood_stress_coupling, mood_trend_declining, stress_mood_strain, high_strain
Trends: Sleep decreasing, Stress increasing, Mood decreasing, Sleep variability moderate, Mood variability low

insights.physicalInsight: Your body is feeling the weight of stress today, making everything feel a bit more demanding. Slowing down isn't just a suggestion — it's essential for your well-being right now.
insights.mentalInsight: With stress levels elevated, your focus is likely to wane, making decisions feel more challenging than they should. This is why clarity drops when stress rises.
insights.emotionalInsight: Everything feels a bit more overwhelming today, as stress is amplifying your emotional sensitivity. Small things are taking more effort, and it’s okay to acknowledge that.
insights.whyThisIsHappening: Stress has been climbing recently, and it's driving how you feel right now, overshadowing the natural hormonal shifts of this luteal phase.
insights.solution: Take 20 minutes to wind down tonight — this will help break the cycle of stress and fatigue.
insights.recommendation: Try to lighten your obligations over the next few days; your body is already working harder than it appears.
insights.tomorrowPreview: You're just 2 days away from your period — most people feel better within a day of their period starting, so hang in there.

description: High energy, high mood, peak state

hormoneContext.narrativeContext: The LH surge typically peaks around ovulation, accompanied by an estrogen peak. Progesterone begins rising as the body shifts into the luteal phase. This window is often associated with high energy, social confidence, and physical vitality.

reasoning: No strong personal baseline deviation detected
Trends: Sleep stable, Stress stable, Mood increasing, Sleep variability low, Mood variability low

insights.physicalInsight: Your energy is high right now — your body is in a strong, well-supported state. Movement and focus feel effortless, making it a great time to tackle tasks that require your full attention.
insights.mentalInsight: Clarity and focus are at their peak — ideas flow more easily and conversations feel smoother. Decisions come naturally and you feel more in control.
insights.emotionalInsight: Things feel lighter and more enjoyable — it's easier to connect with people right now. This is your cycle at its highest point, and you find joy in social interactions.
insights.whyThisIsHappening: The LH surge peaks around ovulation, bringing an elevation in estrogen that fuels this lift in energy, confidence, and clarity. As progesterone begins to rise, you're experiencing the vitality that comes with this phase.
insights.solution: Lean into this momentum today — social plans, focused work, or anything that needs your full presence tend to land easier in this window.
insights.recommendation: This is your peak window — use it for whatever needs your best focus or presence this week. As the post-ovulatory shift approaches, start thinking about how to protect your energy.
insights.tomorrowPreview: Tomorrow marks the transition into the luteal phase, so your energy and mood may start to shift as you move closer to your next period in 14 days.

insights.physicalInsight: Your sleep has dropped sharply over the last few days — around 5.5h, noticeably less than your usual. This is why your body feels off balance and physically low right now.
insights.mentalInsight: Focus drops when sleep dips like this — even simple tasks require more effort, leaving you feeling a bit foggy.
insights.emotionalInsight: Everything feels like it takes more effort than it should — small things feel harder, and your mood is lower than your usual neutral.
insights.whyThisIsHappening: This isn't about your cycle — the recent drop in sleep is what's driving how you feel right now.
insights.solution: Prioritize getting your sleep back on track — that will shift how everything feels more than anything else.
insights.recommendation: Keep your load lighter until your sleep recovers — your energy will come back quickly once it does.
insights.tomorrowPreview: You're 19 days from your period, and ovulation is close — this is where things tend to click into place.

description: Sleep low, stress high, mood fluctuating

hormoneContext.narrativeContext: Progesterone is typically dominant in this window, while LH and FSH tend to stay low. Estrogen has a secondary rise then begins to fall. This progesterone influence is often linked to the calmer, more inward energy of the luteal phase.

reasoning: No strong personal baseline deviation detected
Insight priority drivers: sleep_variability_high, sleep_trend_declining, high_strain
Trends: Sleep decreasing, Stress stable, Mood stable, Sleep variability high, Mood variability moderate

insights.physicalInsight: Your sleep has been inconsistent and lower than your usual 5.5 hours, which is making your body feel more strained today. This is why slowing down isn't optional right now — it's what helps you navigate this phase.
insights.mentalInsight: Focus drops when sleep dips like this, and maintaining clarity feels more challenging. Everything takes more effort, and that’s okay.
insights.emotionalInsight: You feel like small things are harder than they should be, and you're not imagining it — this phase is genuinely harder. It’s normal to feel overwhelmed by everyday tasks right now.
insights.whyThisIsHappening: The progesterone that's dominant in this phase brings a calmer, inward energy, but it also amplifies sensitivity and lowers your capacity to cope with stress. This hormonal shift is contributing to how you're feeling today.
insights.solution: Pick a consistent bedtime and stick to it for the next three nights — that regularity will do more than extra hours.
insights.recommendation: This week, keep your sleep consistent and reduce your load — stress lands harder in this phase.
insights.tomorrowPreview: You're 9 days from your period — tomorrow should feel a bit lighter as your body starts to shift towards the next phase.
